---
title: "Home"
page-layout: full
---

:::::: home-hero
# Connor Conkey-Morrison

::: home-subtitle
PhD Candidate \| Provisional Psychologist \| Sessional Academic
:::

::: home-intro
Research on school attendance problems, emotionally based school avoidance, and adolescent mental health. My work draws on intervention evidence, longitudinal modelling, and population-level education data.
:::

::: home-buttons
[Research & publications](research.qmd){.hero-btn} [Teaching & Roles](teaching.qmd){.hero-btn} [CV & Contact](contact.qmd){.hero-btn}
:::
::::::

::: home-section
## Profile

I am a PhD candidate at RMIT University and a provisional psychologist. My research focuses on school attendance problems, emotionally based school avoidance, and adolescent mental health, with particular interests in intervention evidence, developmental patterns of disengagement, and the use of large-scale administrative and longitudinal data.

Alongside research, I work across teaching, student support, and school-based practice.
:::

::::::: home-grid
::: home-card
### Research & Publications

Current work on school attendance problems, intervention evidence, and developmental trajectories; see peer-reviewed publications.

[View research →](research.qmd)
:::

::: home-card
### Teaching & Roles

Teaching, counselling, and broader academic and professional roles.

[View teaching & roles →](teaching.qmd)
:::

::: home-card
### Contact information

Current appointments, education, and a downloadable CV.

[View Contact information →](contact.qmd)
:::
:::::::
